title: pyrus × kovalevii
intro: Pyrus × kovalevii, also known as the Kovalev's pear, is a hybrid species of pear tree (Pyrus communis) originating from Russia. The medicinal properties of Pyrus × kovalevii lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. This plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections. Pyrus × kovalevii can be prepared as a herbal infusion or tincture, often used to treat respiratory issues and soothe sore throats.
